solution: ```class Solution {
public:
    int reverseBits(int n) {
        unsigned int reversed = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 32; ++i) {
            reversed |= ((n >> i) & 1) << (31 - i);
        }
        return reversed;
    }
};```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The approach iterates through each of the 32 bits, extracts the bit at position i, and sets it in the reversed position. This ensures O(1) time and space complexity. Strengths include simplicity and deterministic performance; weaknesses include potential optimizations via bit manipulation intrinsics or lookup tables for frequent calls.
